                        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[FBI raids Georgia elections office as Trump administration seeks voter data from states]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/fbi-raids-georgia-elections-office-as-trump-administration-seeks-voter-data-from-states</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The FBI searched an elections center in Georgia seeking records related to the 2020 election. Baseless claims of voter fraud have been a focus of President Trump since he lost that race, and the raid comes as his administration has requested voter data from states. Liz Landers discussed more with two secretaries of state, Democrat Shenna Bellows of Maine and Republican Michael Adams of Kentucky.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:55:22 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/fbi-raids-georgia-elections-office-as-trump-administration-seeks-voter-data-from-states</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/2020-election"><![CDATA[2020 election]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/atlanta"><![CDATA[Atlanta]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/donald-trump"><![CDATA[Donald Trump news]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/fbi"><![CDATA[fbi]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/fulton-county"><![CDATA[Fulton County]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/Georgia"><![CDATA[georgia]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/michael-adams"><![CDATA[Michael Adams]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/shenna-bellows"><![CDATA[Shenna Bellows]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/voter-data"><![CDATA[voter data]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Liz Landers]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Ali Schmitz]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Jonah Anderson]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/electionprobe-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[News Wrap: DHS says agents in Alex Pretti shooting on leave]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/news-wrap-dhs-says-agents-in-alex-pretti-shooting-on-leave</link>
            <description><![CDATA[In our news wrap Wednesday, Homeland Security officials say the two federal agents who fired shots in the death of Alex Pretti have been on administrative leave since Saturday, Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended the Trump administration's military operation to seize then-Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and the Federal Reserve hit the brakes on interest rates after three cuts last year.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:50:26 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/news-wrap-dhs-says-agents-in-alex-pretti-shooting-on-leave</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/news-wrap"><![CDATA[news wrap]]></category>
                                                        </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Immigration crackdown fuels tensions as Congress faces shutdown threat]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/immigration-crackdown-fuels-tensions-as-congress-faces-shutdown-threat</link>
            <description><![CDATA[For senators, Wednesday was the first full day back in Washington after the killing of Alex Pretti. It comes ahead of their Friday deadline to fund the Department of Homeland Security and some of the government's other large agencies. They arrived as another lawmaker faced a new threat. Congressional correspondent Lisa Desjardins reports.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:45:11 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/immigration-crackdown-fuels-tensions-as-congress-faces-shutdown-threat</guid>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Lisa Desjardins]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Kyle Midura]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/politicalfallout-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[U.S. cardinal urges defunding of ICE: 'We need to see what's happening in front of us']]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/u-s-cardinal-urges-defunding-of-ice-we-need-to-see-whats-happening-in-front-of-us</link>
            <description><![CDATA[One of the country's highest-ranking Catholic leaders, and a top ally of Pope Leo, is sharply criticizing the Trump administration's immigration enforcement, calling ICE a "lawless organization." Cardinal Joseph Tobin, the head of the Archdiocese of Newark, urged members of the Church to pressure lawmakers to block funding for ICE. Geoff Bennett spoke with Cardinal Tobin to discuss his concerns.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:40:46 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/u-s-cardinal-urges-defunding-of-ice-we-need-to-see-whats-happening-in-front-of-us</guid>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Geoff Bennett]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Karina Cuevas]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/immigrationcrackdown-1024x685.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="685" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Detainees held at Texas ICE facility protest living conditions and treatment]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/detainees-held-at-texas-ice-facility-protest-living-conditions-and-treatment</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Two Democratic members of Congress visited the ICE family detention center in Dilley, Texas, where five-year-old Liam Ramos is being detained, along with many other children and families. Over the weekend, hundreds inside the facility protested their conditions and treatment in a display of defiance. Amna Nawaz spoke with immigration attorney Eric Lee, who was at the center that day.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:35:00 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/detainees-held-at-texas-ice-facility-protest-living-conditions-and-treatment</guid>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Amna Nawaz]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Doug Adams]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Amalia Huot-Marchand]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/familydetention-1-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[George Washington's vision for American identity tested 250 years later]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/george-washingtons-vision-for-american-identity-tested-250-years-later</link>
            <description><![CDATA[As the country observes the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, Judy Woodruff is starting a new chapter of America at a Crossroads. She'll explore what it means to be an American, how that idea has evolved since the nation's founding and where we might be headed. She begins by looking at how closely our identity as Americans adheres to the template created by George Washington.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:30:18 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/george-washingtons-vision-for-american-identity-tested-250-years-later</guid>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Judy Woodruff]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sam Weber]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sarah Clune Hartman]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/crossroads-1024x526.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="526" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Miles O'Brien joins Geoff Bennett to discuss the Challenger disaster on 'Settle In']]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/miles-obrien-joins-geoff-bennett-to-discuss-the-challenger-disaster-on-settle-in</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Forty years ago, the Space Shuttle Challenger exploded on live television just 73 seconds after lifting off. All seven astronauts aboard died, plunging the nation into mourning. On our video podcast "Settle In," Geoff Bennett and Miles O'Brien discussed that moment and how it changed the country.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:25:45 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/miles-obrien-joins-geoff-bennett-to-discuss-the-challenger-disaster-on-settle-in</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/miles-obrien"><![CDATA[Miles O'Brien]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/nasa"><![CDATA[NASA]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/settle-in"><![CDATA[Settle In]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/space-shuttle"><![CDATA[Space shuttle]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/space-shuttle-challenger"><![CDATA[space shuttle challenger]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Geoff Bennett]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Maea Lenei Buhre]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/settlein-2-1024x519.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="519" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Huge landslide leaves Sicilian homes teetering on cliff edge]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/huge-landslide-leaves-sicilian-homes-teetering-on-cliff-edge</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The disaster has forced the evacuation of over 1,500 people and civil protection crews warn the entire hill the town is perched on is falling down.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 18:04:57 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/huge-landslide-leaves-sicilian-homes-teetering-on-cliff-edge</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/italy"><![CDATA[italy]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/landslide"><![CDATA[landslide]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Nicole Winfield, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/AP26027395537674-1024x682.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="682" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Budapest's liberal mayor charged for organizing banned Pride event]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/budapests-liberal-mayor-charged-for-organizing-banned-pride-event</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Authorities in Hungary on Wednesday filed charges against Budapest's liberal mayor over his role in organizing a banned LGBTQ+ Pride event in the capital last year.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 17:47:06 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/budapests-liberal-mayor-charged-for-organizing-banned-pride-event</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/budapest"><![CDATA[Budapest]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/gergely-karacsony"><![CDATA[Gergely Karácsony]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/hungary"><![CDATA[hungary]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/lgbtq-rights"><![CDATA[LGBTQ rights]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Justin Spike, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2025-06-28T144445Z_820646426_RC2QBFAWEA37_RTRMADP_3_HUNGARY-LGBT-PRIDE-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Sarah Mullally confirmed as archbishop of Canterbury, first woman to lead the Church of England]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/sarah-mullally-confirmed-as-archbishop-of-canterbury-first-woman-to-lead-the-church-of-england</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The worldwide Anglican Communion, which includes the Episcopal Church in the U.S., has no formal head, but the archbishop traditionally has been seen as its spiritual leader.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 17:41:05 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/sarah-mullally-confirmed-as-archbishop-of-canterbury-first-woman-to-lead-the-church-of-england</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/church-of-england"><![CDATA[Church of England]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/sarah-mullally"><![CDATA[Sarah Mullally]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Danica Kirka, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/AP26028455333948-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Mississippi sends 135 snowplows to help clear ice and snow from traffic-clogged highways]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/mississippi-sends-135-snowplows-to-help-clear-ice-and-snow-from-traffic-clogged-highways</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Mississippi officials sent National Guard troops, tow trucks and 135 snowplows Wednesday to clear snow and ice from two interstate highways where massive traffic jams began piling up a day earlier on the frozen, slippery roadways.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 17:14:46 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/mississippi-sends-135-snowplows-to-help-clear-ice-and-snow-from-traffic-clogged-highways</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/mississippi"><![CDATA[mississippi]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/tennessee"><![CDATA[Tennessee]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/winter-storm"><![CDATA[Winter storm]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Russ Bynum, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sophie Bates, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Jeff Martin, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/AP26028688752576-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Two companies supplied dried milk powder linked to botulism in ByHeart baby formula]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/health/two-companies-supplied-dried-milk-powder-linked-to-botulism-in-byheart-baby-formula</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The source of the contamination, however, is not yet known. Both companies and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration emphasized that the investigation into the unprecedented outbreak that has sickened 51 babies in 19 states is ongoing.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 17:05:15 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/health/two-companies-supplied-dried-milk-powder-linked-to-botulism-in-byheart-baby-formula</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/baby-formula"><![CDATA[baby formula]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/botulism"><![CDATA[Botulism]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/byheart-baby-formula"><![CDATA[ByHeart baby formula]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[JoNel Aleccia, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/AP26023710699389-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Bruce Springsteen pens new Trump protest song, 'Streets of Minneapolis']]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/arts/bruce-springsteen-pens-new-trump-protest-song-streets-of-minneapolis</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The song, released Wednesday, describes how "a city aflame fought fire and ice 'neath an occupier's boots," which Springsteen calls "King Trump's private army."]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 17:01:15 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/arts/bruce-springsteen-pens-new-trump-protest-song-streets-of-minneapolis</guid>

                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Family of Alex Pretti retains lawyers who helped prosecute the George Floyd case]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/family-of-alex-pretti-retains-lawyers-who-helped-prosecute-the-george-floyd-case</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The parents of Alex Pretti have retained a former federal prosecutor who helped Minnesota's attorney general convict the police officer who kneeled on George Floyd's neck of murder.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 16:55:09 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/family-of-alex-pretti-retains-lawyers-who-helped-prosecute-the-george-floyd-case</guid>

                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minneapolis"><![CDATA[minneapolis]]></category>

                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[2 federal agents involved in Alex Pretti shooting have been on administrative leave since Saturday, DHS says]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/2-federal-agents-involved-in-alex-pretti-shooting-have-been-on-administrative-leave-since-saturday-dhs-says</link>

                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Trump endorses Republican Rep. Tom Tiffany in Wisconsin's open race for governor]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/trump-endorses-republican-rep-tom-tiffany-in-wisconsins-open-race-for-governor</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Trump announced the endorsement in a social media post on Tuesday night, saying Tiffany "has always been at my side."]]></description>

                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Minneapolis sees little change on the streets despite Trump suggesting an easing of tensions]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/minneapolis-sees-little-change-on-the-streets-despite-trump-suggesting-an-easing-of-tensions</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Immigration enforcement operations and confrontations with activists continued Wednesday in Minneapolis and St. Paul.]]></description>

                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[What to know about a trial that will test tech giants' liability for child social media addiction]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/what-to-know-about-a-trial-that-will-test-tech-giants-liability-for-child-social-media-addiction</link>
            <description><![CDATA[A California woman is accusing major social media companies of designing their products to be addictive, especially to children.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Wed, 28 Jan 2026 16:18:19 -0500</pubDate>
